title: 4 Workflow Options for Business Taxes
body_text:
4	Workflow Options for Business Taxes
4.1	Overview of Workflow Options for Business Taxes
Your financial institution can choose from the following two workflows to process remittance vouchers in Business Taxes:
Single workflow
â Front-line staff accept the remittance voucher from your customer and process it in Business Taxes while your customer is present.
Combined workflow
â Front-line staff accept the remittance voucher from your customer and back-office staff process the voucher in Business Taxes.
If the remittance voucher isnât available for processing on Business Taxes, mail or courier the remittance voucher and payment to CRA. See Chapter 9, Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually.
4.2	Single Workflow Processing
This section provides procedures for the single workflow where front-line staff process CRA remittance vouchers directly in Business Taxes. These procedures apply to all CRA remittance vouchers that are accepted for electronic processing. Proceed as follows:
Obtain the CRA remittance voucher from your customer.
Identify the form number (e.g., RC159) and the corresponding tax program (e.g., Payroll Source Deductions) on the remittance voucher. See Chapter 6, Examining Remittance Vouchers.
Important
:	If the voucher is a GST 34 or 62 form, make a copy of the remittance voucher
after
your customer has signed the applicable fields.
To determine if the remittance voucher is acceptable for electronic processing, log in to Business Taxes and search for the form. See Section 8.2, Submitting Files and Tax Payments to CRA via Business Taxes.
Note:	If the voucher isnât available in Business Taxes, continue with procedures in Chapter 9, Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually.
Verify that all form fields on the paper remittance voucher are complete and ensure that your customerâs ID or account number (e.g., 123456782RC0001) is on the remittance voucher.
Debit your customerâs account for the amount on the remittance voucher and credit the appropriate internal GL account for the same amount.
Stamp the remittance voucher and the stub with your financial institutionâs date stamp in the designated areas.
Important
:	If the voucher is a GST 34 or 62 form, stamp the voucher and the photocopy, and record the remittance accepted date on both.
Note:	Payments received outside of regular business hours are treated as the next dayâs business and must be date-stamped accordingly.
Log in to Business Taxes and enter the remittance voucher information. See Section 8.2, Submitting Files and Tax Payments to CRA via Business Taxes, for procedures. Central 1 forwards electronically submitted files and transactions to CRA and the Receiver General.
Separate the remittance voucher from its stub. Give the stub to your customer and print a copy of the Business Taxes receipt for your customer.
Store or dispose of the remittance voucher securely as per your internal procedures.
4.3	Combined Workflow Processing
This section provides procedures for the combined workflow where front-line staff accept CRA remittance vouchers from customers, and back-office staff process the vouchers in Business Taxes. These procedures apply to all CRA remittance vouchers that are accepted for electronic processing.
Procedures for Front-line Staff
Obtain the CRA remittance voucher from your customer.
Identify the form number (e.g., RC159) and the corresponding tax program (e.g., Payroll Source Deductions) on the remittance voucher. Chapter 6, Examining Remittance Vouchers.
Important
:	If the voucher is a GST 34 or 62 form, make a copy of the remittance voucher
after
your customer has signed the applicable fields.
To determine if the remittance voucher is acceptable for electronic processing, log in to Business Taxes and search for the form. See Section 8.2, Submitting Files and Tax Payments to CRA via Business Taxes.
Note:	If the voucher isnât available in Business Taxes, continue with procedures in Chapter 9, Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually.
Verify that all form fields on the paper remittance voucher are complete and ensure that your customerâs ID or account number (e.g., 123456782RC0001) is on the remittance voucher.
Debit your customerâs account for the amount on the remittance voucher and credit the appropriate internal GL account for the same amount.
Stamp the remittance voucher and the stub with your financial institutionâs date stamp in the designated areas.
Important
:	If the voucher is a GST 34 or 62 form, stamp the voucher and the photocopy, and record the remittance accepted date on both.
Note:	Payments received outside of regular business hours are treated as the next dayâs business and must be date-stamped accordingly.
Separate the remittance voucher from its stub and give the stub to your customer.
Important
:	If the remittance voucher is a GST 34 or 62 form, also give the photocopy to your customer.
Provide the remittance voucher to the back-office staff for processing in Business Taxes.
Procedures for Back-office Staff
Review the remittance voucher and verify that it is complete and stamped.
Log in to Business Taxes and enter the remittance voucher information. See Section 8.2, Submitting Files and Tax Payments to CRA via Business Taxes. Central 1 forwards electronically submitted files and transactions to CRA and the Receiver General.
Store or dispose of the remittance voucher securely per your financial institutionâs internal procedures.
END
